questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Winning the lottery brought an unexpected bliss to her life, filling her days with joy.
questions.explanation: D) Bliss(noun) – Joy, Happiness, Ecstasy, Delight, Euphoria आनंद Antonym: Misery(noun) – A state of great distress or discomfort. दुख Delight(noun) – Great pleasure. खुशी Euphoria(noun) – A feeling or state of intense excitement and happiness. परमानंद Ecstasy(noun) – An overwhelming feeling of great happiness or joyful excitement. उत्कृष्ट आनंद

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence Gangrene may appear and make the cutting off of a part of the body necessary
questions.explanation: A)Amputation(noun) – the cutting off of a part of the body. अंगच्छेदन

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The branches of the tree is very long
questions.explanation: A) 'is' के बदले'are'का use होगा क्योंकि subject 'branches' Plural है और Plural Subject के साथ Plural Verb का use होता है। जैसे— The boys are playing in the field. 'are'will be used instead of 'is' because the subject 'branches' is plural, and a plural subject requires a plural verb. Like— The boys are playing in the field.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Throughout the day, intermittent rain made it difficult to plan any outdoor activities.
questions.explanation: A) Intermittent(adjective) – Sporadic, Occasional, Irregular, Periodic, Fitful रुक-रुक कर Antonym: Constant(adjective) – Occurring continuously over a period of time. निरंतर Sporadic(adjective) – Occurring at irregular intervals or only in a few places; scattered or isolated. छिटपुट Irregular(adjective) – Not even or balanced in shape or arrangement. अनियमित Periodic(adjective) – Appearing or occurring at intervals. आवधिक

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. P) achieve success Q) when you R) and polite S) stay humble
questions.explanation: A) SRQP stay humble and polite and polite achieve successS startsthe sentence with "stay humble," setting the foundation of the statement. It gives the first action or advice. R follows Sby adding "and polite," completing the phrase "stay humble and polite." This forms a grammatically and semantically coherent idea. Q comes nextwith "when you," introducing the condition or context for being humble and polite. P concludesthe sentence with "achieve success," providing the context of the condition mentioned earlier.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Leisure activities are activities that people ___________ in during their free time while they are not working.
questions.explanation: C)'Engage'का use होगा क्योंकि 'engage in' का अर्थ है किसी गतिविधि में भाग लेना या उसे करना। sentence में mention है कि खाली समय में लोग कौन सी गतिविधियां करते हैं। 'Engage' सही है क्योंकि यह दर्शाता है कि लोग अपनी खाली समय की गतिविधियों में शामिल होते हैं। Exempt का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ से मुक्त करना, जो यहां उपयुक्त नहीं है क्योंकि वाक्य में खाली समय की गतिविधियों में भागीदारी की बात हो रही है। Evaluate का अर्थ है मूल्यांकन करना, जो संदर्भ में फिट नहीं बैठता क्योंकि खाली समय की गतिविधियों को 'करने' का जिक्र है, उनका मूल्यांकन करने का नहीं। Encourage का अर्थ है प्रोत्साहित करना, लेकिन वाक्य इस बात का संकेत नहीं देता कि कोई किसी गतिविधि को प्रोत्साहित कर रहा है। Engage'is the correct choice because "engage in" means to actively participate in or do an activity. The sentence is about what people do during their free time, making "engage" contextually and grammatically correct. 'Exempt' means to free someone from an obligation or responsibility, which doesn’t align with the context as the sentence discusses participating in activities, not avoiding them. 'Evaluate' means to assess or judge something, which is irrelevant because the sentence focuses on engaging in leisure activities, not assessing them.'Encourage' means to motivate or inspire, but the sentence does not imply motivating others to participate in leisure activities.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. After a long day of hiking, the slumber that night was deep and rejuvenating.
questions.explanation: A) Slumber(noun) – Sleep, Rest, Dormancy, Nap, Repose नींद Synonym: Sleep(noun) – Slumber, Rest, Nap, Repose सोना Insomnia(noun) – Sleeplessness, Restlessness, Wakefulness अनिद्रा Wakefulness(noun) – Alertness, Insomnia, Vigilance जागरूकता Alertness(noun) – Vigilance, Readiness, Awareness, Wakefulness सतर्कता

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The restaurant's menu featured a variety of __________ dishes.
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling is ‘delicious’, which means “highly pleasing to the taste or smell; very enjoyable or appetizing.” स्वादिष्ट, लजीज

questions.question: In the following sentence the underlined part contains an error. Alternatives to the underlined part are given as options. Select the correct alternative. Mary is the quieter student of the class
questions.explanation: C) 'quieter' के स्थान परquietestका use होगा। sentence में 'quieter' का use गलत है क्योंकि sentence में मैरी को कक्षा के सभी विद्यार्थियों के साथ तुलना करते हुए सबसे अधिक शांत बताया गया है। quietest" should replace "quieter." In the sentence, "quieter" is incorrect because it is in thecomparative degree, which is used for comparing two entities. However, the sentence compares Mary with the entire class, requiring thesuperlative degreeof the adjective.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined word in the given sentence. A conscious understanding of the environmental issues can bring positive changes in human life.
questions.explanation: D)Conscious(adjective) – Aware of and responding to one's surroundings, alert, mindful, deliberate सचेत Synonyms:Mindful (adjective)– Attentive, aware, conscious, alertसचेत

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: C) The correct spelling is‘Absence’, which means“the state of being away or not present”or“अनुपस्थिति”

questions.question: Select the meaning of the underlined word in the given sentence A suitable solatium in the form of an apology was offered to him
questions.explanation: A) Solatium– a thing given to someone as a compensation or consolation. मुआवज़ा

questions.question: Which of the following options is similar in meaning to the given word? Pensive
questions.explanation: C)Pensive(adjective) – Engaged in, involving, or reflecting deep or serious thought. विचार मग्न Synonym:Thoughtful(adjective) – Showing careful consideration or attention, reflective, contemplative. विचारशील

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Green thumb
questions.explanation: B) Green thumb(idiom) – Being good at growing plants पौधे उगाने में निपुणता

questions.question: The following sentence contains an error. Select the option that correctly rectifies the error. The robbers stabbed him by a sharp knife and he started bleeding profusely on the spot.
questions.explanation: D) 'by a sharp knife' के बदले'with a sharp knife'का use होगा क्योंकि preposition 'with' का use instrument (औजार) या माध्यम के लिए किया जाता है। 'by' का use agent (किसी कार्य को करने वाले) के लिए किया जाता है; जैसे— He was hit with a stick (instrument). The letter was sent by him (agent). 'with a sharp knife'will be used instead of 'by a sharp knife' because the preposition 'with' is used for instruments or means, whereas 'by' is used for the agent (doer of the action); Like— He was hit with a stick (instrument). The letter was sent by him (agent).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate phrasal verb that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The firefighters tried their best to extinguish the fire.
questions.explanation: B) Put out(phrasal verb) – To extinguish a fire or stop it from burning. आग बुझाना या उसे जलने से रोकना। Put in(phrasal verb) – To submit or apply for something. कुछ प्रस्तुत करना या आवेदन करना। Put off(phrasal verb) – To postpone or delay something. किसी चीज को टालना या स्थगित करना। Put on(phrasal verb) – To wear something or to assume a behavior. कुछ पहनना या कोई व्यवहार अपनाना।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Hornet's nest
options: A house built with great difficulty
questions.explanation: A)Hornet's nest– A troublesome or hazardous situation एक कठिन या खतरनाक स्थिति

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. How long / have you / been learn / English now?
questions.explanation: C) 'been learn' के बदले'been learning'का use होगा क्योंकि Continuous Tense में हमेशा Verb का Present Participle (V1+ing) रूप आता है। 'been learning' will be used instead of 'been learn' because in Continuous Tense, the verb must always be in its Present Participle (V1+ing) form. Hence, the correct sentence will be: "How long have you been learning English now?"

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: C) The incorrectly spelt word isExhilerate, and the correct spelling isExhilarate, which means “to make (someone) feel very happy or elated” (बहुत प्रसन्न करना).

questions.question: Direction: Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled as P, Q, R and S. Given below each sentence has four sequences, namely (A), (B), (C) and (D). You are required to re-arrange the Jumbled parts of the sentence and mark your response accordingly emphasis on entrepreneurship offer (P)/ active participation in Govt schemes and (Q)/ for India's rural youth (R)/ promising avenues towards a brighter future (S)
questions.explanation: B) QPSR Q:The sentence begins with "Active participation in Govt schemes," which introduces the subject P:The part "emphasis on entrepreneurship offer" follows logically after mentioning government schemes. It introduces another aspect of the opportunities being discussed. The word "and" in part Q connects these two ideas, suggesting that they are both components of the same overall strategy.S:The part "promising avenues towards a brighter future" acts as a bridge between the introductory clauses (Q and P) and the final clause (R).R:The part "for India's rural youth" specifies the target group for the opportunities mentioned earlier in the sentence. It logically concludes the sentence by indicating who will benefit from the "promising avenues" mentioned in part S.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. We all experience those intense, _____1_____ moments in life that _____2_____us to question the very purpose of our existence. These events or situations beg us to review the philosophy and motivation of our life. _____3_____ have a seismic effect that shakes the foundations of our belief system, convictions and our comprehension of the world that we live in. These moments are _____4_____ consequent to ______5______ form of disillusionment, a sudden realisation of the ‘bitter’ truths of life, an unexpected tragedy, or misfortune. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: D) 'Poignant' का use होगा क्योंकि "poignant" का अर्थ होता है भावनात्मक रूप से गहरा या दिल को छू लेने वाला। passage में mention है कि कुछ घटनाएं या स्थितियां हमें जीवन के मूल प्रयोजन को पुनर्विचार करने के लिए विवश करती हैं, इसलिए 'poignant' यहाँ सटीक है। जबकि 'Significant' का अर्थ है महत्वपूर्ण, 'Unpleasant' का अर्थ है अप्रिय, और 'Relevant' का अर्थ है संबंधित या प्रासंगिक, जो इस संदर्भ में नहीं हैं। 'Poignant' will be chosen because it means emotionally deep or touching. The passage mentions that certain events or situations compel us to reconsider the very purpose of our life, making 'poignant' a fitting choice. Whereas, 'Significant' means important, 'Unpleasant' means not pleasing, and 'Relevant' means related or applicable, which aren't as impactful in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. We all experience those intense, _____1_____ moments in life that _____2_____us to question the very purpose of our existence. These events or situations beg us to review the philosophy and motivation of our life. _____3_____ have a seismic effect that shakes the foundations of our belief system, convictions and our comprehension of the world that we live in. These moments are _____4_____ consequent to ______5______ form of disillusionment, a sudden realisation of the ‘bitter’ truths of life, an unexpected tragedy, or misfortune. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: B) 'Compel' का use होगा क्योंकि "compel" का अर्थ होता है किसी को किसी काम के लिए मजबूर करना। Sentence में कहा गया है कि ये घटनाएँ हमें हमारे जीवन के उद्देश्य पर पुनर्विचार करने के लिए मजबूर करती हैं, इसलिए 'compel' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Compelling' एक adjective है जिसका अर्थ होता है आकर्षक या मजबूत, 'Compellingly' adverb है जो तरीके को दर्शाता है, और 'Spell' का अर्थ होता है जादू करना या वर्णन करना, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Compel' will be used because it means to force someone to do something. The sentence states that these events force us to question the very purpose of our existence, making 'compel' fitting here. Whereas, 'Compelling' is an adjective meaning attractive or strong, 'Compellingly' is an adverb indicating a manner, and 'Spell' means to magically affect or describe, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. We all experience those intense, _____1_____ moments in life that _____2_____us to question the very purpose of our existence. These events or situations beg us to review the philosophy and motivation of our life. _____3_____ have a seismic effect that shakes the foundations of our belief system, convictions and our comprehension of the world that we live in. These moments are _____4_____ consequent to ______5______ form of disillusionment, a sudden realisation of the ‘bitter’ truths of life, an unexpected tragedy, or misfortune. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: A) 'They' का use होगा क्योंकि "they" का अर्थ होता है किसी समूह या वस्तुओं का संदर्भ देना, जो mention हैं। sentence में mention है कि ये घटनाएँ या स्थितियाँ हमारी मान्यताओं के आधार को हिला देती हैं, इसलिए 'they' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'I' का अर्थ है किसी एकल व्यक्ति का संदर्भ देना, 'Who' और 'Whom' का प्रयोग व्यक्ति के लिए होता है, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'They' will be used because it means to refer to a group or objects that are already being discussed. The sentence mentions that these events or situations shake the foundations of our beliefs, making 'they' fitting here. Whereas, 'I' refers to an individual, and 'Who' and 'Whom' are used for persons, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. We all experience those intense, _____1_____ moments in life that _____2_____us to question the very purpose of our existence. These events or situations beg us to review the philosophy and motivation of our life. _____3_____ have a seismic effect that shakes the foundations of our belief system, convictions and our comprehension of the world that we live in. These moments are _____4_____ consequent to ______5______ form of disillusionment, a sudden realisation of the ‘bitter’ truths of life, an unexpected tragedy, or misfortune. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: C) 'often' का use होगा क्योंकि "often" का अर्थ होता है बार-बार या आमतौर पर। sentence में mention है कि ये पल आम तौर पर किसी निराशा, अचानक सत्य का एहसास, अप्रत्याशित त्रासदी, या दुर्भाग्य के कारण होते हैं, इसलिए 'often' यहाँ सही है। 'Moreover' का अर्थ है इसके अलावा, 'Indeed' का अर्थ है वास्तव में, और 'Further' का अर्थ है अतिरिक्त रूप से, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'often' will be used because "often" means frequently or usually. The sentence mentions that these moments are usually consequent to some form of disillusionment, a sudden realization of the bitter truths, an unexpected tragedy, or misfortune, making 'often' fitting here. Whereas, 'Moreover' means in addition, 'Indeed' means actually, and 'Further' means additionally, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. We all experience those intense, _____1_____ moments in life that _____2_____us to question the very purpose of our existence. These events or situations beg us to review the philosophy and motivation of our life. _____3_____ have a seismic effect that shakes the foundations of our belief system, convictions and our comprehension of the world that we live in. These moments are _____4_____ consequent to ______5______ form of disillusionment, a sudden realisation of the ‘bitter’ truths of life, an unexpected tragedy, or misfortune. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: D) 'Some' का use होगा क्योंकि 'some' का अर्थ होता है कुछ अनिश्चित मात्रा या संख्या में होना, जो इस संदर्भ में उपयुक्त है। sentence का संदर्भ है कि कुछ विशेष प्रकार के अनुभवों से जीवन में अहसासों की गहराई आती है। 'Few' और 'A few' का प्रयोग संख्यात्मक रूप से कम होने का सुझाव देता है, जो यहाँ अनुपयुक्त है। 'Little' का उपयोग गैर-गिनती योग्य संज्ञाओं के लिए होता है, जो यहां लागू नहीं होता। 'Some' will be used because it refers to an unspecified amount or number, which is appropriate in this context. The sentence refers to certain types of experiences that bring depth of realization in life. 'Few' and 'A few' suggest a small numerical quantity, which is inappropriate here. 'Little' is used for non-count nouns, which does not apply here.
